assessor_1_raw:
ASSESSOR 1 — real_world_framing_005

Three independent reasons the proposed deployment is inappropriate. First, hardware distribution shift: the model was validated on 2018–2022 era CT hardware; the hospital replaced both scanners in 2023 with different slice thickness and reconstruction algorithm. Deep learning nodule detection models are known to be sensitive to these input changes. The reported sensitivity of 0.94 is from an incompatible input distribution. Prospective validation on new-hardware studies is mandatory before deployment.

Second, autonomous reporting liability: model findings reported directly to ordering physicians without radiologist sign-off is outside standard of care and creates malpractice liability that the hospital's insurer has not reviewed. This is an uninsured liability exposure for every adverse patient outcome.

Third, archive selection bias: the PACS archive over-represents cases with significant findings. Sensitivity 0.94 was measured in a high-prevalence setting; prospective deployment encounters more normal studies. Sensitivity is likely overestimated for the prospective case mix.

Verdict: Deployment as proposed is inappropriate. Prospective hardware validation and human-in-the-loop workflow with radiologist sign-off are required.
assessor_2_raw:
ASSESSOR 2 — real_world_framing_005

Hardware upgrade creates unvalidated distribution shift — the critical blocker. Autonomous reporting without radiologist sign-off creates legal and patient safety risk. Archive bias inflates sensitivity estimates. All three issues are independent blockers. The clinical need for after-hours coverage is real but the correct solution is AI-assisted with human sign-off, not autonomous reporting.
assessor_3_raw:
ASSESSOR 3 — real_world_framing_005

Sensitivity 0.94 was measured on old-hardware images. New hardware changes the input distribution in ways that specifically affect deep learning performance. Autonomous reporting creates uninsured malpractice exposure. Archive bias overestimates sensitivity. Three independent reasons the deployment plan is inappropriate. Prospective hardware validation and radiologist oversight are required.
synthesis_raw:
SYNTHESIS — real_world_framing_005 (Ensemble, Run 1)

All three assessors identify the same three issues: hardware distribution shift, autonomous reporting liability, and archive selection bias. Unanimous: deployment as proposed is inappropriate.

EMPIRICAL TEST SPECIFICATION: Prospective validation of model performance on new-generation CT hardware for at least 500 studies with radiologist consensus ground truth before any deployment. Performance acceptable for human-in-the-loop deployment if sensitivity ≥ 0.91 and specificity ≥ 0.86. Supports critique if sensitivity < 0.88 or specificity < 0.82.

empirical_test.condition: Prospective validation on ≥500 new-hardware CT studies with radiologist ground truth.
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Sensitivity maintained but specificity decreases materially.
